name: plug-in-sample-colorize
blurb: Colorize image using a sample image as a guide
help: This plug-in colorizes the contents of the specified (gray) layer with the help of a  sample (color) layer. It analyzes all colors in the sample layer. The sample colors are sorted by brightness (== intensity) and amount and stored in a sample colortable (where brightness is the index) The pixels of the destination layer are remapped with the help of the sample colortable. If use_subcolors is TRUE, the remapping process uses all sample colors of the corresponding brightness-intensity and distributes the subcolors according to their amount in the sample (If the sample has 5 green, 3 yellow, and 1 red pixel of the  intensity value 105, the destination pixels at intensity value 105 are randomly painted in green, yellow and red in a relation of 5:3:1 If use_subcolors is FALSE only one sample color per intensity is used. (green will be used in this example) The brightness intensity value is transformed at the remapping process according to the levels: out_lo, out_hi, in_lo, in_high and gamma The in_low / in_high levels specify an initial mapping of the intensity. The gamma value determines how intensities are interpolated between the in_lo and in_high levels. A gamma value of 1.0 results in linear interpolation. Higher gamma values results in more high-level intensities Lower gamma values results in more low-level intensities The out_low/out_high levels constrain the resulting intensity index The intensity index is used to pick the corresponding color in the sample colortable. If hold_inten is FALSE the picked color is used 1:1 as resulting remap_color. If hold_inten is TRUE The brightness of the picked color is adjusted back to the original intensity value (only hue and saturation are taken from the picked sample color) (or to the input level, if orig_inten is set FALSE) Works on both Grayscale and RGB image with/without alpha channel. (the image with the dst_drawable is converted to RGB if necessary) The sample_drawable should be of type RGB or RGBA

args.name: sample-drawable
args.description: Sample drawable (should be of Type RGB or RGBA)

args.name: orig-inten
args.description: TRUE: hold brightness of original intensity levels, FALSE: Hold Intensity of input levels

args.name: rnd-subcolors
args.description: TRUE: Use all subcolors of same intensity, FALSE: Use only one color per intensity

args.name: guess-missing
args.description: TRUE: guess samplecolors for the missing intensity values, FALSE: use only colors found in the sample

If use_subcolors is TRUE, the remapping process uses all sample colors of the corresponding brightness-intensity and distributes the subcolors according to their amount in the sample (If the sample has 5 green, 3 yellow, and 1 red pixel of the  intensity value 105, the destination pixels at intensity value 105 are randomly painted in green, yellow and red in a relation of 5:3:1 If use_subcolors is FALSE only one sample color per intensity is used. (green will be used in this example) The brightness intensity value is transformed at the remapping process according to the levels: out_lo, out_hi, in_lo, in_high and gamma The in_low / in_high levels specify an initial mapping of the intensity. The gamma value determines how intensities are interpolated between the in_lo and in_high levels. A gamma value of 1.0 results in linear interpolation. name: plug-in-sel2path
blurb: Converts a selection to a path
help: Converts a selection to a path

args.name: align-threshold
args.description: If two endpoints are closer than this, they are made to be equal.

args.name: corner-always-threshold
args.description: If the angle defined by a point and its predecessors and successors is smaller than this, it's a corner, even if it's within 'corner_surround' pixels of a point with a smaller angle.

args.name: corner-surround
args.description: Number of points to consider when determining if a point is a corner or not.

args.name: corner-threshold
args.description: If a point, its predecessors, and its successors define an angle smaller than this, it's a corner.

args.name: error-threshold
args.description: Amount of error at which a fitted spline is unacceptable. If any pixel is further away than this from the fitted curve, we try again.

args.name: filter-alternative-surround
args.description: A second number of adjacent points to consider when filtering.

args.name: filter-epsilon
args.description: If the angles between the vectors produced by filter_surround and filter_alternative_surround points differ by more than this, use the one from filter_alternative_surround.

args.name: filter-iteration-count
args.description: Number of times to smooth original data points.  Increasing this number dramatically --- to 50 or so --- can produce vastly better results. But if any points that 'should' be corners aren't found, the curve goes to hell around that point.

args.name: filter-percent
args.description: To produce the new point, use the old point plus this times the neighbors.

args.name: filter-secondary-surround
args.description: Number of adjacent points to consider if 'filter_surround' points defines a straight line.

args.name: filter-surround
args.description: Number of adjacent points to consider when filtering.

args.name: keep-knees
args.description: Says whether or not to remove 'knee' points after finding the outline.

args.name: line-reversion-threshold
args.description: If a spline is closer to a straight line than this, it remains a straight line, even if it would otherwise be changed back to a curve. This is weighted by the square of the curve length, to make shorter curves more likely to be reverted.

args.name: line-threshold
args.description: How many pixels (on the average) a spline can diverge from the line determined by its endpoints before it is changed to a straight line.

args.name: reparametrize-improvement
args.description: If reparameterization doesn't improve the fit by this much percent, stop doing it. Amount of error at which it is pointless to reparameterize.

args.name: reparametrize-threshold
args.description: Amount of error at which it is pointless to reparameterize.  This happens, for example, when we are trying to fit the outline of the outside of an 'O' with a single spline. The initial fit is not good enough for the Newton-Raphson iteration to improve it.  It may be that it would be better to detect the cases where we didn't find any corners.

args.name: subdivide-search
args.description: Percentage of the curve away from the worst point to look for a better place to subdivide.

args.name: subdivide-surround
args.description: Number of points to consider when deciding whether a given point is a better place to subdivide.

args.name: subdivide-threshold
args.description: How many pixels a point can diverge from a straight line and still be considered a better place to subdivide.

args.name: tangent-surround
args.description: Number of points to look at on either side of a point when computing the approximation to the tangent at that point.

name: python-fu-histogram-export
blurb: Exports the image histogram to a text file (CSV)
help:
Exports the image histogram to a text file,
so that it can be used by other programs
and loaded into spreadsheets.

The resulting file is a CSV file (Comma Separated
Values), which can be imported
directly in most spreadsheet programs.

The first two columns are the bucket boundaries,
followed by the selected columns. The histogram
refers to the selected image area, and
can use either Sample Average data or data
from the current drawable only.;

The output is in "weighted pixels" - meaning
all fully transparent pixels are not counted.

Check the gimp-histogram call
embedding_text:
Export Histogram
python fu histogram export

name: python-fu-palette-sort
blurb: Sort the colors in a palette
help:
Sorts a palette, or part of a palette.
Sorts the given palette when it is editable, otherwise creates a new sorted palette.
The default is a 1D sort, but you can also sort over two color channels
or create a 2D sorted palette with sorted rows.
You can optionally install colormath (https://pypi.python.org/pypi/colormath/1.0.8)
to GIMP's Python to get even more channels to choose from.
authors: João S. O. Bueno, Carol Spears, David Gowers
copyright: João S. O. Bueno, Carol Spears, David Gowers

args.name: slice-expr
args.description:
Format is 'start:nrows,length' . All items are optional.

    The empty string selects all items, as does ':'
    ':4,' makes a 4-row selection out of all colors (length auto-determined)
    ':4' also.
    ':1,4' selects the first 4 colors
    ':,4' selects rows of 4 colors (nrows auto-determined)
    ':3,4' selects 3 rows of 4 colors
    '4:' selects a single row of all colors after 4, inclusive.
    '3:,4' selects rows of 4 colors, starting at 3 (nrows auto-determined)
    '2:3,4' selects 3 rows of 4 colors (12 colors total), beginning at index 2.
    '4' is illegal (ambiguous)

embedding_text:
Sort Palette
python fu palette sort
Sort the colors in a palette

Sorts a palette, or part of a palette.
Sorts the given palette when it is editable, otherwise creates a new sorted palette.
The default is a 1D sort, but you can also sort over two color channels
or create a 2D sorted palette with sorted rows.
You can optionally install colormath (https://pypi.python.org/pypi/colormath/1.0.8)
to GIMP's Python to get even more channels to choose from.

Parameters: run-mode: The run mode; palette: Palette; selections: Selections; slice-expr: 
    Format is 'start:nrows,length' . All items are optional.

    The empty string selects all items, as does ':'
    ':4,' makes a 4-row selection out of all colors (length auto-determined)
    ':4' also.
    ':1,4' selects the first 4 colors
    ':,4' selects rows of 4 colors (nrows auto-determined)
    ':3,4' selects 3 rows of 4 colors
    '4:' selects a single row of all colors after 4, inclusive.
    '3:,4' selects rows of 4 colors, starting at 3 (nrows auto-determined)
    '2:3,4' selects 3 rows of 4 colors (12 colors total), beginning at index 2.
    '4' is illegal (ambiguous)
; channel1: Channel to sort; ascending1: Ascending; channel2: Secondary Channel to sort; ascending2: Ascending; quantize: Quantization; pchannel: Partitioning channel; pquantize: Partition quantization
